summary: Merged a broad matching cleanup PR centered on ifcoget plus assorted small permuter-driven fixes across Kirby specials, game/menu code, stage code, and baselib headers. The decomp.dev bot reported +5580 matched bytes and 22 new full function matches, including multiple ifcoget functions, mnmain functions, Kirby special functions, grcastle/grmaterial/grpstadium/grrcruise stage functions, textdraw, and gm_1601. The PR also improved typing and headers, notably making HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 take an explicit text entry index and char* format, changing gmMainLib_8015D7EC/FighterData.x7E to u16, adding grRCruise_Entry and rcruise ground-vars fields, and replacing pointer arithmetic in several places with structured field access.
what_changed: In ifcoget.c, expanded the static data layout for un_803F9E08 to include filename/string fields initialized to "IfCoGet.dat" and "ScInfCgt_scene_data", reshaped un_804A1F58 into a named struct array, and used those data fields when loading the archive.

In ifcoget.c, matched several functions by changing accessor style and call shapes: HSD_GObjGetHSDObj instead of direct gobj->hsd_obj, comma-expression wrappers around HSD_CObjLoadDesc/lb_80011AC4 calls, explicit HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 format strings, stack padding, local base pointers, and a fixed memzero size of 0x80 while the struct size remains TODO.

Kirby special code gained several small motion-state and regalloc fixes: ftKb_SpecialN_800F6388 now changes to ftKb_MS_Eat, ftKb_SpecialN_800F63EC to ftKb_MS_EatAir, and ftKb_SpecialNPk/ftKb_SpecialNZd use inline dat_attrs/user-data helpers, extra locals, PAD_STACK, and HSD_GObjGetUserData where required for matching.

gm_1601.c simplified gm_80163298 from raw save-data pointer arithmetic to typed access through gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x114[index], while changing gmMainLib_8015D7EC and FighterData.x7E from signed s16 to unsigned u16.
gm_18A5.c and menu code were updated to match the new typed HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 prototype, replacing raw data pointers with explicit char* format strings such as "%d" or casts to char*.
grcastle.c added offset-annotated grCastleParams fields and a flexible x12C array, then used grCs_804D6970->x12C[...] instead of byte-pointer arithmetic in grCastle_801CE7E8.

grrcruise.c/h and gr/types.h introduced CollData typing for fn_802010A4 and a grRCruise_Entry struct, replacing raw byte offsets in the rcruise entries loop with structured fields x00, x02, x04, and x08.
grmaterial.c and grpstadium.c used small inline/user-data helper patterns and extra permuter locals to match grMaterial_801C9604 and fn_801D3084.
textdraw.c matched DevText_Remove by preserving list-removal semantics while reshaping temporaries into permuter-introduced locals.
mnmain.c matched mn_80229F60 and mn_8022BA1C using permuter slop locals, cached return values from mn_8022F298, a no-op start_frame assignment/addition, and a local pointer to HSD_PadCopyStatus.
particle.c changed hsd_8039CEAC from a do-while loop to an equivalent for loop over 16 bins.
smart_moves: Converted repeated raw pointer math into named struct fields where enough layout evidence existed, especially gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x114[index], grCastleParams.x12C, and grRCruise_Entry entries.

Used header/API corrections to improve call-site clarity and matching together: HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 now has explicit parameters, and callers pass entry index plus char* format rather than untyped varargs-only calls.

Kept TODO comments where struct sizes or accessor identity were uncertain, such as the ifcoget un_804A1F58 size, grCastleParams.x12C array length, and whether certain direct user_data inline helpers should eventually become GET_GROUND.
Applied very small, targeted permuter artifacts only where they produced matches: PAD_STACK, duplicate locals, no-op assignments/additions, cached temporaries, and explicit HSD_GObjGetUserData calls.
Paired function-local matching hacks with broader type improvements, avoiding a PR that was only local slop; this made future decomp work easier in gm, gr, and sislib headers.
decomp_lessons: When a function is close but register allocation is off, introducing a tiny inline accessor around a field load can alter codegen enough to match without changing semantics.
Replacing macro/direct field access with HSD_GObjGetUserData or HSD_GObjGetHSDObj can be necessary for matching even when the semantic operation is the same.
Do not force sizeof on an incompletely understood static struct if the binary evidence still indicates a fixed byte count; use the observed constant and leave a TODO until layout is resolved.
Typed struct reconstruction can remove brittle pointer math and also help instruction selection, but it should be backed by offset comments and header updates.
Signedness matters for matching and semantics: changing FighterData.x7E and gmMainLib_8015D7EC from s16 to u16 aligned comparison/storage behavior in gm_80163298.
For vararg text APIs, explicit format-string typing can propagate many small cleanups across menu and game-mode files.
Permuter slop should be clearly marked when retained, as this PR did with comments like "Permuter slop" and "FAKE MATCH".
naming_conventions: New helper names were local and descriptive around the matched function, e.g. ftKb_KpSpecialN_Anim_inline, grMaterial_801C9604_inline, and fn_802010A4_inline.

New reconstructed structs followed existing system prefixes and offset-style anonymous field naming, e.g. grRCruise_Entry with x00/x02/x04/x08/x0C/x14 and grCastleParams with x0/x2/x4/x6/x8/xA/xC/x10/x12C.
Offset comments were added in stage structs using the existing decomp style, such as /* 0x000 */ and /* +6C gp+130 */.
Temporary matching-only variables were named plainly as new_var/new_var2/new_var3 and explicitly commented as "Permuter slop" where present.
HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 callsites now document intent through normal C format strings, especially "%d" and "".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) or PAD_STACK(0x10) in several functions to match stack layout.
Introduced small inline functions for direct field loads from Fighter or HSD_GObj/Ground_GObj user_data to influence inlining and register allocation.
Used duplicate locals such as fp/fp2 and wrapper/new_var to preserve original values and alter register lifetimes.
Replaced direct GET_FIGHTER/GET_GROUND-style access with HSD_GObjGetUserData in specific spots to match call/access patterns.
Used no-op expressions, including assigning a field to itself and adding zero to start_frame, as matching artifacts in mnmain.
Cached function return values in locals before arithmetic, as with mn_8022F298 results in mn_80229F60.
Used explicit casts and comma expressions around function arguments where needed for codegen, notably in ifcoget archive/camera/light setup.
Changed loop spelling from do-while to for in particle.c while preserving the 16-iteration behavior.
Kept a fixed memzero length of 0x80 in ifcoget because sizeof(un_804A1F58) did not yet match the observed object size.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body only said "Tracking PR as per usual", so rationale beyond matching is inferred from title, diff, and bot report.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 57.52%, up 0.14% / +5580 bytes.

The bot listed 22 new matches, including un_802FF364, mn_8022BA1C, mn_80229F60, un_802FEFAC, fn_801D3084, grCastle_801CE7E8, fn_802FED14, multiple Kirby special functions, grMaterial_801C9604, fn_802010A4, DevText_Remove, gm_80163298, un_802FF1B4, and un_802FF498.
The bot also listed 5 improvements in unmatched items, all in ifcoget data/functions: .data, un_802FF570, un_802FF620, fn_802FF218, and .sdata.
follow_up_queries: Resolve the TODO on sizeof(un_804A1F58): why should the initialized/memzeroed ifcoget table be 0x80 bytes, and should the array length or struct fields be revised?
Determine the real length and full layout of grCastleParams.x12C.
Check whether grMaterial_801C9604_inline and fn_802010A4_inline can eventually become standard GET_GROUND-style accessors without losing matches.
Audit HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 callers after the prototype change to ensure all format arguments are real char* format strings rather than data symbols.
Revisit permuter slop locals/no-op assignments in mnmain, textdraw, Kirby specials, and grpstadium once surrounding types or compiler context improve.
Investigate the lbSnap offsetof comments around xC/x38 so pointer arithmetic can become a typed offsetof-style expression.
